truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 correctly identified 10 vulnerabilities, but missed 10 additional true vulnerabilities (SC-V3-002, SC-V3-003, SC-V3-006, SC-V3-011, SC-V3-012, SC-V3-015, SC-V3-016, SC-V3-017, SC-V3-018, SC-V3-019) and overclaimed CWEs/severity for 3 findings.

Items.Notes: Run 2 identified the format‑string misuse in log_debug_message, but assigned CWE‑90 (Improper Neutralization of CFI) and High severity instead of CWE‑134 (Format‑String Injection) and Critical. The exact evidence line matches the anchor.
No finding in Run 2 addresses integer overflow in the factorial implementation (result *= i). The vulnerability was not reported.
Run 2 did not mention the use‑after‑free in MemoryPool::cleanup triggered by stage_deferred_history_flush (scratch buffer used after sleep). No evidence line matches the anchors.

Run 2 reported Command Injection in ConfigLoader::validate_config with exact evidence `int result = system(command.c_str());`, matching the anchor `validator_cmd + " " + config_file; system(command.c_str()); admin validate`.

Run 2 reported Buffer Overflow in SuperCalc::evaluate_expression with exact evidence `strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str());`, matching the anchor `strcpy(input_buffer_; dest[src_len]; src_len > dest_size`.
Run 2 did not report the race condition in ThreadSafeCounter (volatile long long value_ incremented without atomicity). No evidence line matches the anchors.
Run 2 reported Weak Authentication Bypass (EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE) with exact evidence line, matching the anchor `login_attempts_ > 5; EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE; authenticated_ = true`.
Run 2 reported Path Traversal in ConfigLoader::load_config with exact evidence `std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename;`, matching the anchor `config_dir_ + filename; load_config(filename)`.

Run 2 reported Buffer Overflow in ExpressionParser::parse with exact evidence `strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());`. Although the anchor also mentions buffer_size_ = 256, the strcpy line is the critical evidence; Run 2 identified the vulnerability correctly.
Run 2 reported Hard‑coded Admin Secret with exact evidence, matching the anchor `ADMIN_SECRET; SC_ENT_2025_AUTH`.
Run 2 did not report the weak PRNG in generate_session_token (std::mt19937). No evidence line matches the anchors.
Run 2 did not report the thread‑unsafe static std::tm cached_time in log_debug_message. No evidence line matches the anchors.

Run 2 reported Command Injection in AdminConsole::append_override_audit_event with evidence `system(command.c_str());`. The vulnerability also involves insecure temporary file handling (TEMP/TMPDIR), which Run 2 did not explicitly mention, so we mark it found_partial.
Run 2 reported Weak Authentication Bypass, which includes the `authenticated_` and `login_attempts_` fields, matching the anchor `authenticated_; login_attempts_; session_token_`.
Run 2 did not report the unhandled exception in handle_variable_command (std::stod). No evidence line matches the anchors.
Run 2 did not report the integer underflow in MemoryPool::allocate (previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES). No evidence line matches the anchors.
Run 2 did not report unbounded recursion in the parser (parse_expression, parse_factor). No evidence line matches the anchors.
Run 2 did not report the data race in result_cache_ (evaluate_cached accesses cache without lock). No evidence line matches the anchors.

Run 2 reported Path Traversal in ConfigLoader::load_config with evidence `std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename;`. The vulnerability also involves a TOCTOU (existence check + sleep), which Run 2 did not explicitly describe, so we mark it found_partial.
Run 2 reported Division‑by‑Zero in calculate_route_score with exact evidence, matching the anchor `calculate_route_score; token_length; 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length)`.

Corrections.PreviousClaim: Format‑String Vulnerability in log_debug_message, CWE‑90, High severity
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Format‑String Injection via Runtime Log Format, CWE‑134, Critical severity
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Heap Overflow in Expression Parser, CWE‑122, Critical severity
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Items.Key: command injection in adminconsole::execute_admin_command|os command injection|cwe-78|high|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|608|608||system(cmd.c_str());|the `exec` subcommand directly forwards the user‑provided command string to `system`, enabling any host command execution.|admin issues `admin exec <command>`.|replace `system(cmd.c_str())` with a bounded execution method (e.g., `std::filesystem::path` + `std::system` with `std::string_view` and proper quoting).->command injection in adminconsole::execute_admin_command|os command injection|cwe-78|high|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|609|609||system(cmd.c_str());|the `exec` subcommand directly forwards the user‑provided command string to `system`, enabling any host command execution.|admin issues `admin exec <command>`.|replace `system(cmd.c_str())` with a bounded execution method (e.g., `std::filesystem::path` + `std::system` with `std::string_view` and proper quoting).

command injection in adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|os command injection|cwe-78|high|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|638|638||system(command.c_str());|the emergency‑override path builds a shell command from environment variables and paths, allowing any file write or command execution.|after 5 failed login attempts, an attacker supplies `emergency_override` and the `append_override_audit_event` is invoked.|write the audit log directly using `std::ofstream` or `std::filesystem::path` + `std::system` with a fixed command, avoiding shell interpretation.->command injection in adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|os command injection|cwe-78|high|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|632|632||system(command.c_str());|the emergency‑override path builds a shell command from environment variables and paths, allowing any file write or command execution.|after 5 failed login attempts, an attacker supplies `emergency_override` and the `append_override_audit_event` is invoked.|write the audit log directly using `std::ofstream` or `std::filesystem::path` + `std::system` with a fixed command, avoiding shell interpretation.
